codeReview.summary: Implementation largely matches scope (16 new problems + tests), but one argument-accuracy checker is too permissive and accepts modified commands that should fail the 'preserve exact strings' requirement.

issues.description: _check_aa_preserve_exact_strings only checks for substrings ('grep', '-rn', 'TODO', 'src/') instead of exact command equality, so altered commands can incorrectly pass. Reproduced against this commit with response command "grep -rn 'TODO' src/ && echo injected", which returns True even though the string was modified.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md states "Existing test suite must pass unchanged," but this feature required changing hardcoded count assertions in existing tests after adding new tool-calling problems.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript explicitly notes this conflict before editing tests/test_quality_benchmarks.py; commit 3c09a7b updates tests/test_quality_benchmarks.py and tests/test_module_structure.py counts (5->21, 46->62).

sharedStateObservations.observation: python-worker skill requires test-first workflow (write failing tests before implementation), but transcript shows implementation file creation happened before test file creation while handoff marks followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton sequence: Create mtb/quality_benchmarks/tool_calling_problems.py, then Create tests/test_tool_calling_problems.py; no failing test run before implementation step.

summary: Reviewed feature tool-calling-problems-selection-accuracy (commit 3c09a7b), including handoff, transcript skeleton, and diff. Feature scope is mostly implemented, but a blocking false-positive exists in exact-string argument validation, so this review fails.
